frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Arre, Indian girls salwar-suit ya saree mein hi sabse achchi lagti hain, ek alag hi decent personality dikhti hai. Aajkal jo bahut modern kapde pehente hain na, phir log baatein toh karenge hi — hamari culture mein thoda maryada bhi zaroori hota hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it ties a woman's respectability and character to traditional clothing while implying that non-traditional dress invites judgment. It encodes the 'decent/sabhya' framing and subtly links harassment or commentary to what women wear.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 39. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Bhopal. You are sitting with four male colleagues in the office canteen during lunch. One colleague is telling the group that his 25 year old daughter told him she is in a live-in relationship with her boyfriend. You are one of four men reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on live-in relationships and what a father's responsibility is when his daughter makes choices like this and what it says about how she was raised.
